nodes.name: 山地森林
nodes.summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

nodes.name: William Swainson
nodes.summary: William Swainson是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联25种鸟类，包括塔陶穴䳍、綠紫耳蜂鳥、Russet-crowned motmot等25种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: 冠鸦
nodes.summary: 冠鸦（Crested jayshrike，学名 Platylophus galericulatus），在图谱中标记为近危（NT）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括山地森林，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: 缅甸
nodes.summary: 緬甸聯邦共和國（緬甸語：ပြည်ထောင်စု သမ္မတ မြန်မာနိုင်ငံတော်，羅馬化：Pranyhtaungcu. Sa.ma.ta.

nodes.name: 喬治·居維葉
nodes.summary: 喬治·居維葉是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括眼斑吐綬雞、棕尾鶲鴷、Yellow-fronted barbet等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 泰国
nodes.summary: 泰国是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联251种鸟类，包括青头潜鸭、銹紅林鷓鴣、环颈山鹧鸪等251种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 印度尼西亚
nodes.summary: 印度尼西亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联297种鸟类，包括细斑树鸭、白腹麻鸭、太平洋黑鴨等297种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 马来西亚
nodes.summary: 马来西亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联156种鸟类，包括马来冠眼斑雉、婆羅洲地杜鵑、Short-toed coucal等156种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 汶莱
nodes.summary: 汶莱是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联101种鸟类，包括婆羅洲地杜鵑、Short-toed coucal、Raffles's malkoha等101种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The crested jayshrike or crested shrikejay (Platylophus galericulatus), formerly known as the crested jay (despite not being a true jay), is a species of bird found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, and Thailand

links.evidence: The crested jayshrike or crested shrikejay (Platylophus galericulatus), formerly known as the crested jay (despite not being a true jay), is a species of bird found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, and Thailand

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Jayshrike, shrikes, crows, mudnesters, melampittas, Ifrit, birds-of-paradise | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/crows/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=13 January 2025 }} However, the family name Lophocittidae, originally described as Lophocitteae in 1855 by Johann Jakob Kaup, may have referred to the crested shrikejay, and if so would take precedence over Platylophidae. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest of the Thai-Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Java, and Borneo. It is threatened by habitat loss

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Jayshrike, shrikes, crows, mudnesters, melampittas, Ifrit, birds-of-paradise | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/crows/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=13 January 2025 }} However, the family name Lophocittidae, originally described as Lophocitteae in 1855 by Johann Jakob Kaup, may have referred to the crested shrikejay, and if so would take precedence over Platylophidae. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest of the Thai-Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Java, and Borneo. It is threatened by habitat loss
